questions.question: What effect size (SMD) did EPA-major formulations (>60% EPA) produce in the 2019 meta-analysis for depression?

options.text: SMD = -0.10 (negligible effect)
SMD = -0.50 (moderate effect)
SMD = -1.03 (large effect, comparable to some antidepressant medications)
SMD = -2.50 (very large effect)

questions.explanation: EPA-major formulations (>60% EPA in total omega-3 content) produced an SMD of -1.03 in studies using validated depression rating scales — a large effect size that is clinically meaningful and comparable to some antidepressant medications. Pure DHA formulations showed no significant antidepressant effect.

questions.question: Why does EPA have antidepressant effects while DHA does not, despite both being omega-3 fatty acids?

options.text: DHA is toxic to brain cells while EPA is not
EPA works through active anti-inflammatory pathways (resolvin production, IDO inhibition, cytokine reduction) while DHA is primarily a structural membrane component
DHA is not actually an omega-3 fatty acid
EPA and DHA have identical effects; the meta-analysis was flawed

questions.explanation: EPA's antidepressant effects operate through active, functional mechanisms: it is a precursor to pro-resolving mediators, competes with arachidonic acid to reduce inflammatory eicosanoids, inhibits IDO enzyme activation (preserving the tryptophan-to-serotonin pathway), and modulates endocannabinoid signaling. DHA is primarily a structural membrane component with diminishing returns once incorporated.

questions.question: What is the total omega-3 daily intake threshold above which bleeding risk becomes clinically significant?

options.text: 500 mg/day
1000 mg/day
3000 mg/day
10,000 mg/day

questions.explanation: Above 3000 mg total omega-3 per day, the bleeding risk becomes clinically relevant due to antiplatelet effects. Omega-3 supplements should be discontinued 7-10 days before planned surgery, and the combination with anticoagulant or antiplatelet medications requires medical supervision.

questions.question: Why is the triglyceride form of omega-3 supplements preferred over the ethyl ester form?

options.text: Triglyceride form is always cheaper
Ethyl ester form has more EPA per capsule
Triglyceride form is approximately 50-70% better absorbed than ethyl ester form
There is no difference between the two forms

questions.explanation: The triglyceride form is better absorbed than the ethyl ester form, with approximately 50-70% better absorption. Look for 'triglyceride form' or 'rTG' on the label. Additionally, taking omega-3s with a fat-containing meal increases absorption by up to 300%.

questions.question: What does EPA supplementation do to brain N-acetyl-aspartate (NAA) levels, and why does this matter?

options.text: EPA decreases NAA, which helps reduce brain inflammation
EPA has no effect on NAA levels
EPA increases brain NAA levels — a marker of neuronal integrity — suggesting it supports fundamental neuronal recovery, not just symptom suppression
EPA converts NAA into serotonin directly

questions.explanation: EPA supplementation increases brain N-acetyl-aspartate (NAA) levels, a marker of neuronal integrity and mitochondrial function. NAA is consistently reduced in depression and bipolar disorder. Its restoration suggests that EPA supports fundamental neuronal recovery — not merely symptom suppression — reinforcing the importance of sustained, consistent EPA supplementation.

title: Omega-3 Dosing: EPA vs DHA for Mental Health - Quiz
